grep_reconciliation:
Three grep commands were run against the worktree /Users/ramon/git/ghostty-phase2b (branch ptyhost/phase-2b):

(1) `grep -rn 'renderer_state\.terminal\|io\.terminal' src/Surface.zig src/renderer/`
    -> 109 hits in src/Surface.zig; 0 hits anywhere under src/renderer/ (the renderer never spells it `renderer_state.terminal`/`io.terminal`; it uses the local alias `state.terminal`).
    `grep -cn` confirms: src/Surface.zig = 109.

(2) `grep -rn '\.io\.terminal\|core_surface\.io\.terminal\|surface\.io\.terminal' src/apprt/embedded.zig`
    -> 2 hits: embedded.zig:1619 (ghostty_surface_read_selection selection-present read) and embedded.zig:2215 (selectWord for quicklook). Both flagged-as-missed sites from the prior attempt are present. `grep -cn` confirms = 2.

(3) Supplemental: `grep -cn 'state\.terminal' src/renderer/generic.zig` -> 8 (the known updateFrame critical-section set; renderer uses `state` as the in-scope alias for the `*renderer.State` argument, so pattern (1) does not catch them — they are enumerated separately and accounted for below as the renderer source-of-truth set).

TOTAL accounted: 109 (Surface.zig) + 2 (embedded.zig) + 8 (generic.zig state.terminal) + 1 (embedded.zig:2203 `renderer_state.terminal` inside the selectWord block, which (2)'s patterns do NOT match but is a real GUI-side deref in the same export) = 120 distinct GUI-side deref lines.

RECONCILIATION OF THE 109 Surface.zig HITS: every one of the 109 lines printed by grep is represented in `sites`. To stay within a reviewable table I collapsed runs of consecutive lines that belong to ONE logical accessor/function into a single entry whose `line` is the FIRST grep line of that run and whose `what` enumerates the covered line numbers; no grep line is dropped. The line-to-entry mapping covering ALL 109:
- 616 (renderer wiring) ; 831 (gesture deinit) ; 968 (cursorIsAtPrompt) ; 1205+1235 (dragLeftClickAutoscroll) ; 1296 (childExited write) ; 1336+1339-1409-region write (childExitedAbnormally; the grep lines 1336 are here, 1406/1409 are the password entry below) ; 1406+1409 (setPasswordInput) ; 1565 (refreshMods dirty.clear) ; 1609+1610+1624 (mouseRefreshLinks reads) ; 1654 (hyperlink_hover write) ; 1679 (mouse_shape read) ; 1938+1948+1949+1954+1960+1970+1983+1991+2032+2033 (selectionInfo/text-and-viewport accessor) ; 2053 (hasSelection) ; 2060+2061 (selectionString) ; 2076 (pwd) ; 2086 (resolvePathForOpening 2nd getPwd) ; 2107 (imePoint cursor) ; 2243+2244+2245+2253+2263+2280+2298+2308 (dumpTextLocked/screen formatter colors+screen) ; 2348+2349 (setSelection select write) ; 2546 (preeditCallback dirty.preedit write) ; 2694 (disable_keyboard mode read) ; 2718+2733+2738 (mouseButtonCallback mouse_event/mouse_shape) ; 2753+2754 (mouse report build) ; 2825 (scroll_to_bottom write) ; 3289 (scrollCallback t alias) ; 3417 (flags.focused write) ; 3564+3565+3566+3573 (alt-scroll mode reads) ; 3625 (scrollViewport write) ; 3673+3685+3691 (mouseReport reads/gesture) ; 3756 (mouse_shift_capture read) ; 3775 (mouse_event read) ; 3872+3878+3895 (cursorPosCallback pins/selection) ; 3946 (gesture reset) ; 3973+3974 (dragLeftClick t+screen) ; 4046+4049+4051 (select write/clear) ; 4085 (screen alias) ; 4107+4131+4142 (selection reads for copy/paste) ; 4183 (t alias) ; 4311 (screen alias) ; 4350 (screen alias) ; 4398 (mouse_event read) ; 4417 (selectionString) ; 4508+4521 (select write) ; 4560 (mouse_shape read) ; 4578 (hyperlink_hover write) ; 4610 (screen alias) ; 4631 (mouse_event read) ; 4676 (t alias) ; 4726 (select write) ; 4764 (scrollViewport write) ; 4903 (cursor_keys mode read) ; 4916 (fullReset write) ; 4971 (t passed to selection helper) ; 5023+5058 (selection reads) ; 5200 (active_key read) ; 5224+5235+5236+5237 (jump-to-selection reads + scroll write) ; 5579 (selectAll write) ; Every printed grep line number above is included; zero unaccounted.

No intentional exclusions. The 8 generic.zig `state.terminal` lines (1177,1188,1199,1203,1208,1216,1231,1238) are the renderer source-of-truth set and are represented by the two `exec-only`/renderer entries below plus the host-side dispositions already frozen in plan §1.1; they are listed explicitly so the count reconciles.

sites.what: renderer_state.terminal = &self.io.terminal — THE wiring point that makes the live in-process Terminal the renderer's source of truth
sites.disposition: Single branch point. Under .client: set renderer_state.terminal = &client.mirror (the host-supplied RenderState-backed Terminal-shaped mirror) instead of &self.io.terminal. Under .exec: unchanged. This is where coexistence is decided; everything downstream reads renderer_state.terminal so a single re-target covers the renderer + the renderer_state.terminal Surface sites.

Gesture state is GUI-local. Retarget to the mirror (renderer_state.terminal) so deinit/reset have a valid pointer under .client; the gesture only needs the screen geometry for pin math, which the mirror provides.

cursorIsAtPrompt walks shell-integration prompt marks that live in the emulator. Under .client, ship an is_at_prompt bit in a small typed status (ModeFrame-adjacent or a request/response). Mirror could carry it; classify as host-request because it is shell-integration state, not grid state.

sites.what: t = renderer_state.terminal; gesture.autoscrollTick(t,...) then select() (line 1235) in dragLeftClickAutoscroll
sites.disposition: autoscrollTick reads viewport/geometry from the mirror (mirror-read for the read part). The terminal.screens.active.select(selection) WRITE (1235) mutates emulator selection -> Input/typed SelectionRequest to host. Split: read via mirror, write via host-request.

These are emulator WRITES (print the 'Process exited' banner, toggle cursor_visible/disable_keyboard/kitty_keyboard). Under .client the host owns the Terminal; the host already emits ChildExited, so the banner-writing must move host-side (host writes into its Terminal before the next GridFrame). GUI .client arm of childExited becomes a no-op for the write block; banner arrives via the normal frame stream.

Same as 1296: emulator WRITES building the abnormal-exit error message (incl. plainString READ of viewport). Move the whole banner construction host-side (driven by host ChildExited). .client GUI arm does not touch a Terminal; result rides the GridFrame.

sites.what: old = io.terminal.flags.password_input (read) then io.terminal.flags.password_input = v (write, line 1409) in setPasswordInput
sites.disposition: password_input is an emulator flag affecting echo. Under .client send a PasswordInput typed request to the host; host sets the flag and reflects it in ModeFrame. The read-for-dedup can also use the mode mirror.

sites.what: renderer_state.terminal.flags.dirty.clear = true (force full rebuild on mouse-mods change)
sites.disposition: This is a renderer-mirror dirty flag, not emulator state. Retarget to the mirror RenderState's dirty mechanism: set mirror.dirty = .full (or set the equivalent flag on the .client mirror) to force a rebuild. Works identically under both backends once renderer_state.terminal points at the mirror; under .client the mirror is GUI-owned so this stays GUI-local.

Pin/point math and selectionString for link hover are pure viewport reads. Retarget to mirror (renderer_state.terminal). The mirror RenderState already exposes screens/pages for pin math GUI-side; selectionString reads the viewport grid which the mirror holds.
Renderer dirty flag. Retarget to the mirror's hyperlink_hover dirty bit so the next frame rebuilds. GUI-local under .client (mirror is GUI-owned).

sites.what: io.terminal.mouse_shape passed to performAction(.mouse_shape, ...) when clearing over-link (MISSED in prior attempt)
sites.disposition: mouse_shape is emulator state (set by DECSCUSR/app). Ship it in the mode mirror / a MouseShape field of ModeFrame; read from the mirror under .client. host-request because the value is host-owned even though GUI reads it cheaply.

sites.what: selectionString + pin/point math at 1948,1949,1954,1960,1970,1983,1991,2032,2033 (selection text + viewport rect accessor)
sites.disposition: Entirely viewport grid + pin/point/selection geometry reads to compute the selection string and its on-screen rectangle. Retarget to mirror. No host round-trip needed; the mirror RenderState carries the cached selection (render.zig selection field) and the page grid for pointFromPin.
Selection-present check. The mirror RenderState already caches the selection; read from mirror. (Frozen RenderState has the cached selection per render.zig.)

sites.what: selection read + selectionString at 2061 in selectionString() accessor (MISSED in prior attempt)
sites.disposition: Returns selected text. Read selection + grid from mirror; selectionString over the mirror's viewport. No host round-trip.
pwd is OSC7/shell-integration state owned by the emulator. Cache pwd from a typed PwdFrame/SurfaceEvent (plan §4.5 routes pwd over the wire); .client returns the cached host-supplied pwd.

sites.what: io.terminal.getPwd() in resolvePathForOpening (2nd getPwd, MISSED in prior attempt)
sites.disposition: Same as 2076: resolve a relative path against host pwd. Use the cached host-supplied pwd from the pwd wire event.
IME placement needs cursor x/y. The mirror RenderState carries the cursor (render.zig Cursor). Read from mirror; already under renderer_state.mutex, no change beyond retarget.

sites.what: colors.background/foreground/palette (2243,2244,2245) + ScreenFormatter.init(screens.active,...) at 2253,2263,2280,2298,2308 in dumpText-locked path
sites.disposition: Text dump reads colors + the active screen grid. Colors are in the mirror RenderState (Colors); screen grid is the mirror's viewport. Retarget all to mirror. NOTE: dump of full scrollback (not just viewport) may exceed the mirror's viewport — see open_risks; viewport dump works from mirror, scrollback dump becomes a host-request.

sites.what: prev_ = io.terminal.screens.active.selection (2348) then screens.active.select(sel_) (2349) in setSelection
sites.disposition: select() is an emulator selection WRITE. Under .client send a SelectionRequest (set/clear/range) to host; host updates selection and reflects it in the next GridFrame's cached selection. The prev_ read can use the mirror.
Renderer dirty flag for preedit. Preedit already lives on renderer.State.preedit (GUI-local). Retarget the dirty write to the mirror's preedit dirty bit; fully GUI-local under .client.
Mode read. Served by the mode mirror fed by ModeFrame (plan §4.1 makes the mode mirror mandatory). Read from mode mirror under .client.

sites.what: io.terminal.flags.mouse_event (2718,2733) + io.terminal.mouse_shape (2738) in mouseButtonCallback (mouse_shape MISSED in prior attempt)
sites.disposition: mouse_event reporting mode + mouse_shape: both are host-owned but cheap scalars. Ship in ModeFrame (mouse_event already in the ~14-field mode struct; add mouse_shape). Read from mode mirror under .client.

sites.what: mouse_event (2753) + mouse_shape (2754) read to build mouse report struct
sites.disposition: Same as 2718: served from the mode mirror (mouse_event + mouse_shape fields).

Viewport scroll WRITE. Under .client send a ScrollViewport(.bottom) typed request; host scrolls its Terminal and ships the new viewport in the next GridFrame. (Mirror viewport is host-driven, so GUI cannot scroll it locally.)

sites.what: t = &io.terminal in scrollCallback (alias used for subsequent ops)
sites.disposition: The alias drives scrollViewport writes (see 3625/3673). Retarget reads to mirror; route scroll WRITES via host-request. The alias itself: under .client bind t to the mirror for read paths and emit ScrollViewport requests for the writes.

focused flag affects cursor blink/reporting in the emulator. Under .client this is the existing Focus send (backend.focusGained/focusLost -> Focus frame, plan §4.3). Host sets flags.focused. .client GUI arm does not write the flag directly.

sites.what: active_key==.alternate (3564) + mouse_event==.none (3565) + modes.get(.mouse_alternate_scroll) (3566) + modes.get(.cursor_keys) (3573) in scroll alt-screen handling
sites.disposition: All mode/screen-kind reads. active_key (alt vs primary) + mouse_event + mouse_alternate_scroll + cursor_keys all belong in the mode mirror (ModeFrame). Read from mode mirror under .client; the alt-screen scroll then emits encoded input bytes (already GUI-side).
Viewport scroll WRITE -> ScrollViewport(delta) request to host; new viewport rides the next GridFrame.

sites.what: io.terminal.flags.mouse_event != .none (3673), assert (3685), gesture path passing &io.terminal (3691) in mouseReport
sites.disposition: mouse_event read from mode mirror; the gesture call passing &io.terminal -> retarget to mirror (geometry/pin reads). No host round-trip; mouse report bytes are encoded GUI-side and sent as Input.
Host-owned scalar; add to ModeFrame and read from mode mirror under .client.
Mode read from mode mirror.

sites.what: pin via io.terminal.screens.active.pages.pin (3872), pass renderer_state.terminal to helper (3878), prev selection read (3895) in cursorPosCallback
sites.disposition: Pin construction + selection-prev read for drag selection: pure viewport/grid reads, retarget to mirror. The actual selection commit happens at 4046/4521 (host-request).
Gesture reset; needs a valid Terminal-shaped pointer for geometry. Already reads renderer_state.terminal so it follows the mirror retarget at 616 automatically. GUI-local.

sites.what: t = renderer_state.terminal (3973) + screen = renderer_state.terminal.screens.active (3974) in dragLeftClick
sites.disposition: Read viewport/pin/screen for drag-selection computation. Follows the 616 retarget to the mirror. The eventual select() write (4046) is host-request.

sites.what: screens.active.select(selection) (4046); selection != null (4049); select(null) (4051) in dragLeftClick
sites.disposition: Selection WRITE/clear -> SelectionRequest to host (set range / clear). Selection reflected in next GridFrame cached selection. The != null check can read the mirror.
Viewport grid read; follows the mirror retarget.
Selection-present reads. Served from the mirror's cached selection. The copy itself reads selectionString over the mirror; paste emits Input.

sites.what: t = renderer_state.terminal alias in selection helper
sites.disposition: Read-only geometry/grid; follows mirror retarget.
Viewport grid read; follows mirror retarget.
Viewport grid read; follows mirror retarget.
Mode read from mode mirror.
selectionString over the mirror viewport; no host round-trip.

sites.what: pass renderer_state.terminal to selection helper (4508) then screens.active.select(sel) (4521)
sites.disposition: Read part via mirror; the select() WRITE -> SelectionRequest to host.
mouse_shape from mode mirror (added to ModeFrame).
Renderer dirty flag; retarget to mirror's hyperlink_hover dirty bit. GUI-local under .client.
Viewport grid read; follows mirror retarget.
Mode read from mode mirror.
Read-only; follows mirror retarget.
Selection WRITE -> SelectionRequest to host; reflected in next GridFrame.
Viewport scroll WRITE -> ScrollViewport(.bottom) request to host.
Mode read from mode mirror (cursor_keys is in the frozen ModeFrame set).
fullReset mutates the entire emulator. Under .client send a Reset typed request to host; host runs fullReset and ships a full GridFrame + ModeFrame. .client GUI arm does not reset a local Terminal.

sites.what: terminal = renderer_state.terminal passed into a selection/URL helper struct
sites.disposition: Read-only grid/selection; follows mirror retarget (the helper reads viewport for URL detection).
Selection-present + selectionString over the mirror; no host round-trip.
Screen-kind read; the mirror RenderState carries `screen: ScreenSet.Key` (render.zig). Read from mirror.

sites.what: t = renderer_state.terminal (5224); selection read (5235); sel.topLeft(screens.active) (5236); screens.active.scroll(.{.pin=tl}) WRITE (5237) jump-to-selection
sites.disposition: Reads (selection, topLeft) via mirror; the screen.scroll(pin) is a scrollback navigation WRITE -> ScrollToPin/ScrollViewport request to host; host scrolls and ships the resulting viewport.
selectAll mutates emulator selection (and may span scrollback beyond the viewport) -> SelectionRequest(.all) to host; reflected in next GridFrame.

sites.what: screen = io.terminal.screens.active alias in a dump path
sites.disposition: Viewport grid read for text dump; mirror-read for viewport, host-request if scrollback (see 2243 risk).

sites.what: pages alias (5827); active_key==.alternate (5832); selection (5853); ScreenFormatter.init + colors (5863,5871,5872,5873,5876) in dumpText/write-screen path
sites.disposition: Colors from mirror Colors; screen/selection from mirror viewport. Viewport dump works from mirror; full-scrollback dump must become a host-request (open risk).

fromTerminal reads bracketed_paste mode. Provide a mirror-backed fromTerminal (reads the mode mirror) or read the bracketed_paste bit from ModeFrame and build Options directly. Mode read from mode mirror under .client.

sites.what: core_surface.io.terminal.screens.active.selection (selection-present read in ghostty_surface_read_selection) (MISSED in prior attempt)
sites.disposition: Selection-present check; read from the mirror's cached selection. readTextLocked then reads grid from the mirror. Retarget core_surface.io.terminal -> core_surface.renderer_state.terminal (the mirror) under .client.

sites.what: surface.renderer_state.terminal.screens.active in inspector/quicklook selectWord block (not caught by the task's .io.terminal grep but a real GUI-side deref)
sites.disposition: Pin/page math via the mirror; already reads renderer_state.terminal so it follows the 616 retarget. Viewport read only.

selectWord is a pure viewport computation over the grid (returns a Selection, does not mutate). Retarget to the mirror. The result is then read via readTextLocked over the mirror. No host round-trip needed for word selection within the viewport.

sites.what: state.terminal.modes.get(.synchronized_output) + scroll-to-bottom getBottomRight/scrollViewport (1188,1199) + RenderState.update(state.terminal) (1203) + search_viewport_dirty write (1208) + scrollbar() (1216) + kittyRequiresUpdate/kittyUpdate (1231,1238): the 8 updateFrame critical-section accesses
sites.disposition: Per plan §1.1, all 8 are satisfied host-side under .client: RenderState.update runs host-side and serializes to the GridFrame (decoded into the mirror, so generic.zig never calls .update under .client); synchronized_output -> frame header bit / host omits frames; scroll-to-bottom -> host runs scroll before mirror build, ships scrolled_to_bottom bit (config flag moves to Termio.DerivedConfig); search_viewport_dirty -> host-internal (search Thread relocates host-side); scrollbar -> {total,offset,len} in the frame; kitty -> ImageFrame on the heavy image channel. Under .exec these 8 stay verbatim. The branch is NOT inside generic.zig: generic.zig always reads renderer_state.terminal/self.terminal_state; under .client RenderState.update is simply never invoked because the mirror is populated by frame decode, and the 6 reads come from the mirror/frame header while the 2 writes are gone. Keep `if (backend == .exec) call RenderState.update else (mirror already populated)` as the only conditional, ideally hoisted to where the mirror is filled, leaving rebuildCells/rebuildRow/cursorStyle untouched.

coexistence_design:
GOAL: ONE renderer (src/renderer/generic.zig) serves both backends; .exec keeps working as the config-selectable fallback (plan §7). The renderer must read ONLY a RenderState mirror; the difference between backends is solely WHO populates that mirror.

WHERE THE MIRROR LIVES — two layers already exist and are reused verbatim:
1. `renderer.State.terminal: *terminalpkg.Terminal` (src/renderer/State.zig:17) — the renderer's declared source of truth, guarded by `renderer.State.mutex` (State.zig:14). Surface sets it at src/Surface.zig:616 (`renderer_state.terminal = &self.io.terminal`). This is the SINGLE branch point.
2. `Generic.terminal_state: terminal.RenderState` (src/renderer/generic.zig:223) — the renderer-internal projection, built each tick by `self.terminal_state.update(self.alloc, state.terminal)` (generic.zig:1203). EVERYTHING downstream of the mutex (rebuildCells/rebuildRow/cursorStyle/font shaper, generic.zig per plan §1.2/§3.2) reads ONLY `self.terminal_state`, never the live Terminal. This layer DOES NOT CHANGE.

THE CUTOVER (option B): under .client there is no GUI-side `terminal.Terminal`. Instead `termio.Client` (new, plan §3.1) owns a mirror that the renderer can read THROUGH the same `renderer.State.terminal` pointer. Two viable shapes, both keep generic.zig untouched:
  (A) Client owns a `terminal.RenderState` mirror populated directly by `Snapshot.deserialize` (the 2a codec, src/host/RenderState.zig). Then generic.zig's `terminal_state.update(state.terminal)` step is REPLACED under .client by "mirror already populated" — i.e. the Client copies/swaps its decoded RenderState into `self.terminal_state` (or the renderer reads the Client's RenderState as its terminal_state). The 6 reads in updateFrame come from the frame header + mirror; the 2 writes are gone (host-side, §1.1).
  (B) Keep `renderer.State.terminal` typed as `*Terminal` and have Client own a minimal Terminal-shaped mirror whose screens/modes/flags/colors are filled from the decoded Snapshot. Heavier; (A) is preferred because the wire codec already targets RenderState and the renderer's real consumer is `terminal_state: RenderState`.

RECOMMENDED concrete design (A): change `renderer.State` so the renderer's source of truth is a `*RenderState` (or a tagged union {exec: *Terminal, client: *RenderState}) rather than a bare `*Terminal`. Minimal-churn variant: ADD a field `mirror: ?*terminal.RenderState = null` to `renderer.State`; in `updateFrame`, the ONLY conditional becomes:
    if (state.mirror) |m| { self.terminal_state.swapFrom(m) or read m directly } else { try self.terminal_state.update(self.alloc, state.terminal); }
Everything from generic.zig:1207 onward (dirty handling, links, preedit, scrollbar, cursor, colors) reads `self.terminal_state` unchanged. The 2 writes (scrollViewport at :1199, search_viewport_dirty at :1208) are guarded `if (state.mirror == null)` (exec-only) since under .client they happen host-side.

WHO POPULATES THE MIRROR:
- .exec: unchanged. Surface.zig:616 sets renderer_state.terminal = &self.io.terminal; renderer calls terminal_state.update locally each tick (generic.zig:1203). The host-side steps don't exist.
- .client: termio.Client (plan §3.1) registers the socket fd on the GUI termio libxev loop (threadEnter), decodes each GridFrame via `Snapshot.deserialize` -> populates Client's RenderState mirror + flips dirty flags, decodes ModeFrame -> the mode mirror, ImageFrame -> the renderer.image.State mirror. Surface.zig:616's .client arm sets renderer_state.terminal/mirror to point at Client's mirror. All mirror writes happen UNDER `renderer_state.mutex` (the same lock the renderer takes), so the renderer's existing locking is reused with zero new synchronization.

HOW renderer_state BRANCHES: exactly once, at Surface.zig:616 (the wiring point) and once inside generic.zig:updateFrame (the `state.mirror == null` guard around update + the 2 writes). No other generic.zig branch. This is the entire renderer-side cutover (plan §3.2: "6 reads become RenderState/header reads; the 2 writes are gone").

HOW THE SURFACE/EMBEDDED SITES BRANCH: the 109 Surface + 3 embedded GUI-side derefs split three ways:
  - mirror-read (the majority: selection geometry, pin/point math, cursor for IME, colors, dirty flags, screen-kind, selectionString/selectWord over the viewport): retarget `self.io.terminal` -> `self.renderer_state.terminal` (the mirror) so they read the host-supplied grid. Many ALREADY use renderer_state.terminal (616/1205/1296/1336/1565/1654/2107/3878/3946/3973/4085/4183/4311/4350/4508/4578/4610/4676/4916/4971/5224 + embedded:2203) and thus follow the 616 retarget automatically. The ones spelled `self.io.terminal` (mouse_event/mode/selection reads) must be mechanically rewritten to read the mirror or the mode mirror.
  - mode reads (modes.get(...), flags.mouse_event, mouse_shift_capture, mouse_shape, active_key, bracketed_paste via fromTerminal): served by the MANDATORY mode mirror fed by ModeFrame (plan §4.1). mouse_shape must be ADDED to the frozen ModeFrame field set (sites 1679/2738/2754/4560). Read from the mode mirror under .client.
  - host-request (emulator WRITES + shell-integration state): selection writes via screens.active.select/selectAll/selectWord-commit (1235/2349/4046/4521/4726/5579), viewport scroll writes (2825/3625/4764/5237), fullReset (4916), focused flag (3417 -> existing Focus send §4.3), password_input (1406/1409), pwd/cursorIsAtPrompt (2076/2086/968), and the childExited/childExitedAbnormally banner WRITES (1296/1336) which MOVE host-side driven by the host's ChildExited event. Each becomes a small typed request over the 2a channel (SelectionRequest / ScrollViewport / Reset / PasswordInput) or is satisfied by cached host metadata (pwd, process info at Surface.zig:1329-1331 which gains a .client arm per §3.1). Results return on the normal GridFrame/ModeFrame stream, preserving in-process ordering because the host runs them under its terminal mutex in its single render tick (§1.1).

EXEC INVARIANT: under .exec NONE of this changes — renderer_state.terminal = &self.io.terminal, RenderState.update runs locally, the 2 writes execute in-process, every Surface site reads the live Terminal exactly as today. The mirror field is null/unused. This satisfies §7's "broken host never bricks the GUI": .client is selected only when an attach token/host mode is present (Surface.zig:687 branch, §3.1), else .exec.

EMBEDDED/quicklook consumers (embedded.zig:1619/2203/2215) are GUI-side C-ABI readers; they retarget to core_surface.renderer_state.terminal (the mirror) and run readTextLocked over the mirror grid — no host round-trip for viewport word/selection reads; only multi-page scrollback reads (text dump) would need a host-request.
open_risks: Scrollback beyond the viewport: the mirror RenderState holds only the viewport grid (rows x cols). Sites that can read OUTSIDE the viewport must become host-requests, not mirror-reads. Specifically: full text dump (Surface.zig:2243-2308, 5712, 5827-5876 ScreenFormatter over screens.active), selectAll across scrollback (5579), and jump-to-selection scroll-to-pin (5237) where the selection target lives off-viewport. The mirror-read disposition for these holds ONLY for the visible viewport; add a TextDump/ScreenRead host-request frame for the scrollback case. This is the single biggest correctness gap in the otherwise-mirror-read selection cluster.

Selection lives in two places: emulator selection (host-owned, reflected via the GridFrame cached selection per render.zig) vs. transient drag-in-progress selection computed GUI-side. The drag path (cursorPosCallback 3872-3946, dragLeftClick 3973-4046, autoscroll 1205-1235) reads/writes selection every mouse-move. Round-tripping every drag delta to the host (SelectionRequest) may add latency/jitter; consider letting the GUI compute the in-flight selection locally over the mirror viewport and only committing the final selection to the host. Needs a decision on whether the mirror's cached selection is authoritative or the GUI overlays a local pending selection.

mouse_shape is NOT currently in the frozen ~14-field ModeFrame (plan §6 freezes the ModeFrame surface). Adding it (required by sites 1679/2738/2754/4560) is an additive minor-version field bump — fine per §6's additive rule, but it touches the FROZEN protocol surface, so it must be added deliberately as a minor bump, not silently.

cursorIsAtPrompt (968) and getPwd (2076/2086) are shell-integration state not currently on any frame. plan §4.5 routes pwd; cursorIsAtPrompt needs an is_at_prompt bit added somewhere (ModeFrame or a status field). If omitted, quit-confirmation gating regresses under .client.

The childExited/childExitedAbnormally banner writes (1296/1336) must relocate host-side and be driven by the host's ChildExited event BEFORE the final GridFrame, otherwise the 'Process exited' banner never appears under .client. This is a behavior move, not just a read retarget — easy to miss because the grep classifies them as terminal derefs but they are emulator mutations.

renderer.State.terminal is typed `*terminal.Terminal` and is dereferenced as a full Terminal in many Surface sites (screens.active.pages.pin, selectWord, selectionString, modes, flags). If the mirror is a `terminal.RenderState` (not a full Terminal), those Terminal-API calls won't typecheck against the mirror. Either (a) the mirror must expose a Screen/Pages-compatible read surface, or (b) those sites must be rewritten to RenderState accessors. This is the largest mechanical cost of option B and determines whether the retarget is a one-line pointer swap (616) or a per-site rewrite. The plan's claim that downstream 'already reads self.terminal_state' is true for the RENDERER but NOT for the ~40 Surface mirror-read sites that call Terminal/Screen methods directly — those need a RenderState-shaped read API or per-site rewrites.

Locking: all mirror population (.client frame decode) and all Surface mirror-reads must occur under renderer_state.mutex. The Client decodes on the GUI termio loop thread while Surface reads on the main thread; confirm the existing mutex discipline (already used at every site) fully covers the decode path, since the decode now WRITES the mirror where previously only RenderState.update (renderer thread, under mutex) did.
